Albin Review - Empires & Puzzles Holy Legendary Hero Strategy Guide

Don't let the comfy pajamas fool you; Albin is here to reset the battlefield (and your buffs). We dive into why this slow-mana Santa helper might still be the secret ingredient your Rush Attack team needs, despite his aging stats.
Albin, The Cocoa Commander: Sipping Slowly, Hitting Hard
Albin is a 5-star Holy hero from the Winter Family (Santa's Challenge), originally released in December 2024. In an era of hyper-fast ninjas and magic tower sorcerers, Albin takes a more...

approach to warfare. He’s a Slow mana Paladin who specializes in resetting the momentum of battle—quite literally—while serving up a double helping of minions to his team. While he doesn't have a costume to modernize him yet, his unique utility keeps him in the conversation for specific niches. Plus, anyone who brings hot chocolate to a sword fight deserves at least a little respect.

Visually, Albin is the definition of "comfort core." He’s depicted sitting down (a power move in itself), clutching a massive mug of hot cocoa topped with whipped cream and a gingerbread man, surrounded by gifts. The art style is vibrant and festive, leaning heavily into the whimsical side of the Winter event.
Unlike the fierce warriors or terrifying monsters usually gracing our screens, Albin looks like he’s just enjoying a lazy Sunday morning. It’s a hilarious contrast when this cozy little guy nukes the entire enemy team for 440% damage. He’s arguably the most "unbothered" hero in the game.
When Albin launched in late 2024, his stats were considered robust. However, fast-forward to 2026, and the power creep train has left the station.
Decent, but no longer elite. Modern hitters often push 1300+ without breaking a sweat.
His bulk is his saving grace. That HP pool is still massive, allowing him to survive long enough to actually fire his Slow skill.
, he trails significantly behind the latest 2025/2026 heroes who sit comfortably in the
range. He relies on his Paladin class and Limit Breaks to stay competitive in diamond arena play.

"The duration of buffs is reset for all allies." This is his unique selling point. It pairs beautifully with heroes who have powerful but short-duration buffs (like Taunt, Dodge, or heavy Attack boosts).

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter family is notoriously stingy with passives, a point of contention for many players.

The Winter Family bonus boosts minion stats. Since Albin floods the board with minions (up to 10 at once), he actually utilizes this bonus better than almost anyone else in the family. A small boost to 10 minions adds up.
Editor's Note: Yes, it hurts. In an age where heroes have 3-4 passives that cleanse, boost mana, and make coffee, Albin just has his mug. This is his biggest weakness compared to modern heroes.

At the start of battle, +20% Defense for 6 turns.
This is excellent for a Slow hero. It helps mitigate the risk of dying before charging mana. It’s simple, boring, but effective for his role.
Upon release, Albin was seen as a "Rush War specialist" and a niche synergy piece. He never dominated the meta like
, but he found a home in minion-heavy teams.
As of 2026, Albin feels his age. His damage, while high percentage-wise, struggles to dent the 1600+ Defense stats of modern tanks without defense down support.
In standard PvP, he is too slow and lacks the passive protection (like resistance to mana control) to be reliable. However, in
, he remains a B+ tier threat. The buff reset mechanic keeps him unique, ensuring he isn't completely obsolete.
Since Albin is a Paladin, you have a choice to make.
Maximizes his Paladin class talent (Defense boost on damage). Ensures he survives to fire his skill.
His 440% damage will hit like a wet noodle against modern teams.
He is a damage dealer first and foremost. You bring him to wipe the board. maximizing his Attack stat is crucial to making that 440% count.
Slightly squishier, but the Aether Power helps compensate.
. If Albin fires, you want the enemy dead. If he survives but hits for 200 damage, he wasted a slot.
Resetting buff durations is a rare and powerful strategic tool (e.g., extending a 3-turn Taunt or Gazelle's dance back to full duration).
Instantly adding 20% HP to the whole team via minions is great for survivability.
440% AoE is devastating when it happens in 6 tiles.
A death sentence in most modern raid formats.
Vulnerable to ailments, mana cuts, and effects that modern heroes naturally resist.

Can be useful for the buff reset to keep attack buffs active longer (if timed perfectly), though items usually do this better.

Initially, beta testers called him "rubbish" due to low damage. The developers nearly doubled his damage to 440% before release, turning him from a joke into a nuke.
Upon release, players argued whether the Very Fast sniper
was better. History proved that while Ottilia was faster, Albin's impact in Rush was far greater.
"Albin perhaps even hit harder with his best stat – but he needs to be paired well. He pairs very well with Guardian Gazelle... reset Gazelle buff for 4 more turns."

Albin is a hero out of time. His skill set is powerful and unique, but his chassis (speed and stats) is outdated. He’s like a really comfortable, high-quality armchair in a room full of futuristic gaming chairs. He’s not the most efficient, but he does exactly what he promises: gives you a cozy break while the enemy team explodes.
Keep for Rush Attack and Gazelle synergy. Otherwise, let him enjoy his hot chocolate on the bench.
